program_outcomes: Matematik, fen bilimleri, hesaplama ve bilgisayar mühendisliği konularında kuramsal/ uygulamalı bilgilere ve yeterli altyapıya sahiptir.
Karmaşık bilişim problemlerini fark etme, tanımlama, formüle etme ve çözme bilgi ve becerisine sahiptir. Bu amaçla uygun analiz ve modelleme yöntemlerini seçme ve uygulama becerisine sahiptir.

Gereksinimleri belirlemeye yönelik olarak karmaşık bir sistemi, sistem parçasını ya da süreci gerçekçi kısıtlar ve koşullar altında analiz eder, alternatifleri mühendislik yöntemlerini kullanarak kıyaslar, en uygun çözümü tasarlar.

Tasarımın gerçekleştirilmesi için tüm kaynakların verimli kullanılması, modern teknik ve araçların geliştirilmesi, seçilmesi ve kullanılması becerisine sahiptir. Süreç içerisinde deney tasarlama, deney yapma, veri toplama, sonuçları analiz etme ve yorumlama becerisine sahiptir.

Disiplin içi ve disiplinler arası projelerde bireysel, takım üyesi veya takım lideri olarak etkin ve sonuç odaklı çalışır. Proje yönetimi, risk yönetimi ve değişiklik yönetimi hakkında bilgi sahibidir
Bir konuya yönelik olarak global kaynak araştırmalarını yapar, verimli bir şekilde değerlendirir ve kullanır.
Yaşam boyu öğrenmenin ve kişisel gelişimin sürekli farkındalığı ile bilişim teknolojilerindeki güncel gelişmeleri izler. Yenilikleri takip eder, kendini sürekli yeniler, girişimcidir.

Sözlü ve yazılı etkin iletişim kurar, açık ve anlaşılır talimat verir ve alır, etkin sunum yapar. Etkin rapor yazar ve yazılı raporları anlar, tasarım ve üretim raporları hazırlar. İngilizce ve Türkçe kullanarak bilişim alanındaki bilgileri izler, yorumlar ve teknik doküman hazırlar.

Bilişim uygulamalarının; kurumsal, toplumsal ve evrensel boyutlarda; sağlık, çevre ve güvenlik üzerindeki sonuçlarını göz önünde tutar, çağın bilişim problemleri hakkında bilgi sahibidir, sorumluluğunun bilincindedir. Sürdürülebilir kalkınma hakkında bilgi sahibidir.

Mesleki ve etik sorumluluk bilincine sahiptir, bilişim hukuku temel prensiplerini anlar, değerlendirir ve mesleki çalışmalarına uygular. Bilişim uygulamalarında kullanılan standartlar hakkında bilgi sahibidir.

courses.name: INTRODUCTION TO COMPUTER ENGINEERING
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to give the students basic knowledge about computer hardware, computer arithmetic, computer architecture, computer networks, operating systems, programming concepts and script languages. Besides, from the presentations carried out through the semester, the students are expected to get general knowledge about information technology problems and applications.
courses.description: Introduction to computer engineering topics including computer hardware and software basics, computer arithmetic, computer architecture, programming languages and paradigms, computational problem specification and algorithmic solution, operating systems, computer networks and the internet, algorithms, software engineering, computer graphics, artificial intelligence, and contemporary computational problems. The course also includes presentations on related information technology topics.
learning_outcomes: To have basic information about basic computer hardware and software
To solve problems by means of computer arithmetic
To understand basics of computer architecture
To understand the types of programming languages, and the paradigms
To understand a computational problem specification and algorithmic solution

courses.name: DISCRETE COMPUTATIONAL STRUCTURES
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to introduce discrete data structures and to reach a solution by using these structures within a mathematical perspective for the encountered problems.
courses.description: The course covers discrete data structures and mathematical tools used to solve problems. Topics include sets, relations, functions, counting, matrices, graphs, trees, propositional logic, predicate logic, inference rules, and proof methods.

Apply the information about combinatorial mathematics, logic and proof in solving the encountered problems
Analyze the problems within the course subjects from a mathematical perspective
Formulize the problems within the course subjects from a mathematical perspective

courses.name: CAREER PLANNING
courses.purpose: The primary purpose of the course is to improve undergraduate learners' career awareness, to make them aware of the expectations and dynamics of business life, to increase their employability by supporting their personal and professional development.
courses.description: This course covers career-related concepts, the changing nature of career, self-awareness, personal future design, future professions and competencies, communication skills, professional relationship networks, CV and cover letter preparation, interview techniques, and career opportunities in the public sector, private sector, entrepreneurship, academia, and NGOs.
learning_outcomes: Be able to understand career and related concepts, individual career planning process and its importance
Be able to have self-awareness
Be able to understand career options
Be able to grasp future professions and competencies
Be able to use self-expression and communication skills effectively
Be able to apprehend the importance of social capital and professional relationship networks
Be able to reach the right resources and use the resources effectively in the career planning process.

courses.name: CALCULUS I
courses.purpose: The sequence Math 1001-1002 is the standard complete introduction to the concepts and methods of calculus. It is taken by all engineering students. The emphasis is on concepts, solving problems, theory and proofs. Students will develop their reading, writing and questioning skills in Mathematics.
courses.description: Preliminaries, functions, limits and continuity; differentiation and tangent lines; derivative rules and the chain rule; derivatives of trigonometric functions, higher order derivatives, the mean value theorem, implicit differentiation, transcendental functions, inverse functions, exponential and logarithmic functions; natural logarithm and exponential functions, inverse trigonometric functions, hyperbolic functions, related rates; indeterminate forms, extreme values, concavity and inflections; sketching the graph of a function, extreme-value problems, linear approximations; integration, sums and sigma notation, areas as limits of sums, the definite integral, properties of the definite integral, the fundamental theorem of calculus; substitution, areas of plane regions; integration by parts, integrals of rational functions, inverse substitutions; improper integrals, applications of integration; volumes by slicing, arc length and surface area.
learning_outcomes: Interpret a function from an algebraic, numerical, graphical and verbal perspective and extract information relevant to the phenomenon modelled by the function.
Verify the value of the limit of a function at a point using the definition of the limit.
Find points of discontinuity for functions and classify them.
Show whether a function is differentiable at a point.
Find the derivative of elementary polynomials, exponential, logarithm and trigonometric functions.
Interpret the definite integral geometrically as the area under a curve and the volumes of solids using the areas of their cross-sections.

Being able to gain an ability to apply Newton s laws of motion to physical problems

courses.name: TECHNICAL ENGLISH
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to provide the students with the necessary grammar and vocabulary in order to make them capable of writing technical reports and make efficient presentations, as well as acquiring the ethics of computer engineering.
courses.description: Technical vocabulary and terminology of computer engineering; grammar of technical English; structure, outline, contents, and section writing of technical reports and academic papers; preparing and writing technical reports and project progress reports; presentation of technical reports and rules of effective presentations; introduction to engineering, technology, computer engineering, and IT ethics; ethical behavior in computer engineering.
learning_outcomes: Analyze the resources of computer engineering written in english.
Define and describe the topics of computer engineering, in correct words and vocabulary.
Obtain and use the up to date vocabulary and terminology in computer engineering topics.
Write technical reports and literature in technical english.
Make effective english presentations, in computer engineering.

courses.name: LOGIC DESIGN
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to give fundamental digital electronics and analytical techniques used in digital logic to solve problems related to digital logic circuits. It is expected that students at the end of this course will be able to understand the basic structure and operation of a digital computer and be able to design digital logic circuits using gates and other simple digital components.
courses.description: This course covers digital and analog quantities, binary digits, logic levels, digital waveforms, basic logic operations and functions; number systems, operations, codes and conversions; logic gates, Boolean algebra and logic simplification; implementing combinational logic and the universal property of NAND and NOR gates; combinational logic functions such as adders, comparators, decoders, encoders and code converters; multiplexers, demultiplexers, parity generators/checkers; latches and flip-flops; asynchronous and synchronous counters; shift registers; algorithmic state machines; synchronous sequential networks; and interfacing the digital and analog worlds including D/A and A/D conversion.
learning_outcomes: Understand arithmetic in the various number systems
Apply Boolean Algebra to minimize the design of a logic circuit
Use Karnaugh maps to minimize the design of a logic circuit
Design combinational logic circuits
Design minimal finite-state machines
Design synchronous sequential counters
Design logic circuits using or implementing multiplexers, encoders, simple latches and flip-flops
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Digital and analog quantities, Binary Digits, Logic Levels, Digital Waveforms, Basic Logic Operations and functions
Hafta 2: Number Systems, Operations, Codes and Conversions
Hafta 3: Logic Gates, Boolean Algebra and Logic Simplification
Hafta 4: Implementing combinational logic, the universal property of NAND and NOR gates, operation with pulse waveforms.
Hafta 5: Functions of Combinational Logic:Basic adders, parallel binary adders, comparators, decoders, encoders, code converters.
Hafta 6: Multiplexers, demultiplexers, parity generators/checkers.
Hafta 7: Review of logic design topics, Solving sample questions
Hafta 8: Latches, Flip-Flops and operation characteristics of Flip-Flops.
Hafta 9: Flip-Flops applications, Asynchronous and synchronous counter operation, Up/Down synchronous counters
Hafta 10: Design of synchronous counters, cascaded counters, counter decoding and applications
Hafta 11: Shift registers and applications
Hafta 12: Algorithmic State Machines: ASM charts, State assignments, ASM tables
Hafta 13: Syncronous Sequential Networks
Hafta 14: Interfacing the digital and analog worlds, digitalto-analog (D/A) conversion, analog-to-digital (A/D) conversion

courses.name: PHYSICS II
courses.purpose: The major objectives of this course are to provide the fundamental knowledge of electromagnetism, to develop solid and systematic problem solving skills, and to lay the foundations for further studies in natural sciences and engineering.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental topics of electricity and magnetism, including electric fields, Gauss's law, electric potential, capacitance and dielectrics, current and resistance, direct current circuits, magnetic fields, sources of magnetic fields, Faraday's law, and inductance.

Being able to describe the basic concepts of magnetism
Being able to calculate the electric field and magnetic field of a given charge and current distribution
Being able to explain the electrical and magnetic properties of materials
Being able to compute the circuital parameters of elements in basic electrical circuits, and to carry out controlled experiments verifying the results of the calculations
Being able to apply the fundamental principles of electricity and magnetism, together with logical and mathematical reasoning, to situations of the physical world acquire

courses.name: DATA STRUCTURES

courses.description: The course covers object-oriented programming design principles, recursion and efficiency of algorithms, and the implementation and use of fundamental data structures such as priority queues, lists, dictionaries, hash tables, graphs, and trees. Develop new algorithms that involve use of various data structures.
Identify the most suitable data structure for a given real-life problem.

courses.name: INTRODUCTION TO CIRCUITS AND ELECTRONICS
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to present the general methods of circuit analysis and design as well as an introduction to electronic devices and analysis of electronic circuits.
courses.description: Introduction to resistive circuits and power; electric circuit theorems including Kirchhoff's current and voltage laws, serial and parallel circuit elements, Norton and Thevenin equivalents; resistance-capacitor and resistance-inductor circuit equations and analysis; introduction to semiconductors, solid state physics fundamentals and diode circuits; bipolar junction transistor characteristics and circuit analysis; logic gate circuit design using BJTs; introduction to op-amp characteristics and op-amp circuit analysis; introduction to FET semiconductors and SRAM cell design; review and question-answer sessions on circuit and electronics topics.
learning_outcomes: Understands fundamental concepts such as voltage, current, power, energy used in circuit theory
To be able to analyze circuits using Ohm Law, Kirchoffs Laws, Superposition, Norton/Thevenin Equivalents
To be able to analyze Capacitive and Inductive circuits as well as sinusoidal steady state in AC circuits
Understands BJT abstraction and amplification concepts and analyzes BJTcircuits
Analyzes diode and operation amplifier circuits
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Resistive circuits and Power
Hafta 2: Electric circuit Theorems: Kirchoff's current and voltage laws
Hafta 3: Electric circuit Theorems: Serial, parallel circuits elements and circuit analysis
Hafta 4: Electric circuit Theorems: Norton and Thevenin Equivalents
Hafta 5: Resistance and Capacitor, Resistance and Inductor circuit equations and analysis
Hafta 6: Introduction to semi-conductors: solid state physics fundamentals and diode circuits
Hafta 7: Biopolar junction transistors characteristics
Hafta 8: Diode and Biopolar junction transistors circuit analysis
Hafta 9: Circuit design of logic gates using bipolar junction transistors
Hafta 10: Introduction to op-amp characteristics
Hafta 11: Circuit analysis of op-amp circuits
Hafta 12: Introduction to FET semiconductors and SRAM cell design
Hafta 13: Circuit and electronics topics review
Hafta 14: Circuit and electronics questions and answers
resources: Presentations
James W. Nilsson, Susan Riedel, Electric Circuits, 10th ed, Pearson, 2015
Robert Boylestad, Louis Nashelsky, Electronic Devices and Circuit Theory, 11th ed, Pearson, 2014
Laboratory Manual

courses.description: Students work on two projects during the semester. The course focuses on project description and brainstorming, design, implementation, completion, evaluation, and presentation of the projects. It also emphasizes teamwork and integrating the outputs of the term courses.

Design the projects.
Implement the projects by programming language after design.

courses.name: PROBABILITY AND STATISTICS
courses.purpose: To make students learn fundamental concepts of probability and statistics, to describe data sets by graphical and numerical methods, to form probability distributions for both discrete and continuous random variables, to learn sampling distribution and central limit theorem, and to construct confidence intervals and apply hypothesis testing procedures.
courses.description: The course covers the science of statistics, types of statistical applications, fundamental elements of statistics, types of data, collecting data, numerical measures for central tendency and variability, sample spaces and events, discrete and continuous random variables, probability distributions, common discrete and continuous distributions, sampling distributions, the central limit theorem, confidence intervals, and hypothesis testing.
learning_outcomes: Describe fundamental concepts of Statistics
Calculate descriptive statistics summarizing data sets
Calculate probabilities by probability functions of discrete and continuous random variables
Use sampling distributions
Apply central limit theorem
Construct confidence interval for various parameters
Test statistical hypothesis for various parameters
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: The Science of Statistics, Types of Statistical Applications, Fundemantal Elements of Statistics, Types of Data, Collecting Data
Hafta 2: Numerical Measures for Central Tendency
Hafta 3: Numerical Measures for Variability, Numerical measures for Relative Standing
Hafta 4: Sample Spaces and Events, Unions and Intersections, Complementary Events, Additive Rule and Mutually Exclusive Events
Hafta 5: Discrete Random Variables, Probability Distributions
Hafta 6: Continuous Random Variables, Probability Density Functions

Hafta 8: The Normal Distributions, The Normal Approximation to the Binomial Distribution, Exponential Distribution
Hafta 9: Solving Problems Related to Probability and Statistics
Hafta 10: What is Sampling Distribution, The Central Limit Theorem

Hafta 12: Large Sample Confidence Interval for Two Population Means, Large Sample Test of Hypothesis About a Population Proportion, Large Sample Test of Hypothesis About a Population Variance and Hypothesis Testing
Hafta 13: Large Sample Confidence Interval for Two Population Proportions, Large Sample Confidence Interval for Two Population Variances and Hypothesis Testing
Hafta 14: Probability and Statistics Review

courses.name: DIFFERENTIAL EQUATIONS AND LINEAR ALGEBRA
courses.purpose: The goal of this course is to establish the mathematical background about the fundamental concepts, solution methodologies and technical applications of linear algebra and differential equations.
courses.description: This course covers introductory and advanced topics in differential equations and linear algebra, including first-order ordinary differential equations, higher-order linear differential equations, matrix algebra, linear transformations, determinants, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, diagonalization, and systems of linear differential equations.
learning_outcomes: Understand solution methods of first order ordinary differential equations.
Understand the theory and the solutions of higher order linear differential equations with homogeneous and inhomogeneous terms.
Understand matrix algebra.
Analyze linear transformations.
Compute eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a matrix.
Solve systems of linear differential equations by matrix exponential function.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to differential equations, solution curves. First order ordinary differential equations: Seperable differential equations.
Hafta 2: Homogeneous differential equations, linear differential equations.
Hafta 3: Bernoulli type nonlinear differential equations, exact differential equations.
Hafta 4: Integrating factors and non-exact differential equations.
Hafta 5: Existence and uniqueness theorems of first order differential equations. Applications of first order differential equations.
Hafta 6: Matrix Calculus: Matrix operations, transpose of a matrix, Symmetric and skew-symmetric matrices.
Hafta 7: Elementary row operations, inverse of a matrix. Solutions of systems of linear equations by elementary row operations.
Hafta 8: Determinant, properties of determinant. Cramers rule.
Hafta 9: Linear Transformations, image, kernel and associated properties.
Hafta 10: Matrix representations of linear transformations.
Hafta 11: Higher order linear differential equations, linear dependence and independence of solutions, representation of solution spaces.
Hafta 12: Method of Variation of Parameters.
Hafta 13: Eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Diagonalization.
Hafta 14: Matrix formulation of systems of linear differential equations and the solutions of systems of linear differential equations by matrix exponential function.
resources: Lectures on Differential equations, E.Akyıldız, et all, 2012, METU Press.
Differential Equations and Linear Algebra, C.H. Edwards, D.E. Penney, Third Edition, 2010, Pearson.
Linear algebra with applications, S. J. Leon, Eight edition, 2010, Pearson.

courses.name: TURKISH LANGUAGE I
courses.purpose: The objective of Turkish Language Course is to enable the comprehension of the structural and operational features of Turkish; and to enable the acquisition of the proper and fine use of the language which is a means of written and spoken expression.
courses.description: This course introduces Turkish language, its structural and functional features, the relationship between language and culture, the place of Turkish among world languages, the historical periods of Turkish, its phonetic structure, spelling and punctuation rules, the basics of written expression, corporate writing and correspondence types, and scientific/academic writing.
learning_outcomes: To be able to explain the features of language and its importance in human and social life.
To be able to explain the connection between language and culture.
To be able to explain the place of Turkish among the languages around the world.
To be able to explain the information about the historical periods of Turkish.
To be able to have a more sensitive and conscious point of view towards language.
To be able to explain the phonetic structure and features of Turkish.
To be able to explain the characteristics of corporate writing and correspondence types and to create such articles.
To be able to explain the features of scientific and academic writing and to create such kinds of articles.

Hafta 2: Connection between language and culture
Hafta 3: Classification of languages in the world, the place of Turkish among the languages around the world
Hafta 4: Historical periods of the Turkish I (From the Beginning to the Middle Turkish Period)
Hafta 5: Historical periods of the Turkish II (Middle Turkish Period)
Hafta 6: Historical periods of the Turkish III (New and Modern Turkish Periods)

courses.name: RESEARCH METHODOLOGIES
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to teach students research methodologies in Computer Engineering and acquire students the skills of conducting comprehensive research, writing reports, and making presentations.
courses.description: This course covers types of scientific research, topic selection, research planning, avoiding plagiarism, locating and assessing sources, reading sources critically, integrating and critiquing sources, referencing systems, report writing, and presentation techniques.
learning_outcomes: Explaining a current problem related to Computer Engineering
Researching the subject thoroughly from the literature
Reading sources critically
Gaining the ability to prepare written reports on the subject
Gaining the ability to present the subject in front of the public with different presentation techniques

courses.name: INTRODUCTION TO GEOSPATIAL ASSESSMENTS ON ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY
courses.purpose: This course is designed to equip students with the knowledge and skills necessary to leverage geospatial technologies for evaluating, monitoring, and promoting sustainable environmental practices. It aims to help participants integrate GIS, remote sensing, and spatial analysis techniques to address complex environmental challenges and contribute to a more sustainable world.
courses.description: The course covers introductory concepts of environmental sustainability and geospatial technologies, including GIS, remote sensing, spatial data collection and handling, spatial analysis, geo-referencing, coordinate systems, map projections, and applications in environmental monitoring and assessment. It also includes case studies and applications in areas such as water resource management, ecosystem mapping, climate change, social sciences, humanities, and public health.
learning_outcomes: To understand the fundamentals of geospatial technologies and their applications in environmental sustainability.
To learn how to collect, process, and analyze geospatial data for environmental assessments.
To develop proficiency in using GIS software and remote sensing tools for sustainable resource management.
To explore case studies and real-world applications of geospatial assessments in diverse environmental contexts.
To apply geospatial analysis techniques to assess environmental impacts and propose sustainable solutions.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to environmental sustainability concepts and challenges
Hafta 2: Overview of geospatial technologies (GIS, remote sensing, etc.)
Hafta 3: Geospatial data collection and handling; Open-source geospatial data repositories for environmental topics; Data quality and validation considerations
Hafta 4: Geographic Information Systems (GIS) for Environmental Sustainability; GIS applications in environmental conservation and management
Hafta 5: Spatial data analysis for environmental assessments
Hafta 6: Remote Sensing for Environmental Monitoring; Land cover/land use mapping and change detection
Hafta 7: Geo-referencing spatial environmental data; coordinate system, datum and map projection concepts
Hafta 8: Mid-Term Exam; Student assignments for individual project topics
Hafta 9: Geospatial Assessments on Water Resource Management and Hydroclimatic Extremes (Floods, Drought, Heat Waves, etc.)
Hafta 10: Geospatial Applications in Ecosystem Mapping, Restoration and Biodiversity Conservation
Hafta 11: Geospatial Analysis for Climate Change Mitigation, Resilience and Adaptation Issues
Hafta 12: Geospatial Assessments for Social Sciences, Humanities and Public Health Domains
Hafta 13: Case studies and applications from global and European domains
Hafta 14: Presenting student project assignments
resources: Selected course materials will be provided/indicated during the lecture hours.

courses.name: BUSINESS MANAGEMENT AND MANAGEMENT
courses.purpose: Teaching the contemporary management science and techniques, and their implementation in business life consequently prepare students to working life; giving them necessary information in corporate governance environment in everchanging and progressing modern approaches by doing so having them apply these effectively in business.
courses.description: The course covers management theories, management and ethics, entrepreneurship and its management, business organization structures and contemporary approaches, management and decision making mechanisms, management and planning, strategic planning, MBO (management by objectives), project management (GANTT, PERT, CPM with examples), management and communication, management, manager, leadership, organization and groups, management and motivation, conflict management, and managerial approach to changes in organization.
learning_outcomes: Students who take this class are knowledgeable in principal and new management approaches and teach theories and implementation of management.
Student furnished information in management and decision making so they will get skills in right decision making.
Student knows the fundamental principles of business life plan, planning and strategic planning with samples in real life.
Student kmows the contemporary trend of business life of multinational corporations and their working principles and prepared to working environment of global institutions
Knows the organization, Communications, and motivation skills and have to use them in business and daily life.
Learned the aspects of being a manager and leader.

courses.name: CLIMATE CHANGE ASSESSMENTS ON ENVIRONMENTAL ISSUES, PRIORITIES AND CHALLENGES
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of climate change and its impact on the environment. Students will learn about the scientific basis of climate change, its drivers, consequences, and potential solutions. The course will focus on conducting assessments of climate change's effects on various environmental aspects, identifying priorities, and addressing key concerns to foster sustainable development. Prospective students will also collect fundamental knowledge about the conceptual and political development of climate change related strategies and policies at various levels of decision-making and governance structures.
courses.description: Historical development of the sustainability concept, strategies and policy interventions; addressing climate change phenomenon at the transect of environmental sustainability; climate change science and assessment methods; the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), its acts and contributions; climate models and projections for the future; data collection and analysis methods for climate change assessments; impacts of climate change on biodiversity, vulnerability and adaptation of ecosystems; impacts of climate change on water resources and quality, sustainable water management, water scarcity and drought; energy and carbon footprint, the role of energy production in climate change, climate policies for mitigating the carbon impact; urban heat island effect and climate adaptation in cities, green infrastructure and urban planning for resilience; agricultural impacts of climate change, risks on food security; climate change assessments within the Water-Energy-Food-Ecosystem (WEFE) Nexus; Nature-Based Solutions (NBS) against climate change.
learning_outcomes: To understand the scientific basis of climate change, its drivers, consequences
To gain basic capacities for exploring potential solutions against climate change
To contribute for conducting assessments on climate change impacts on various environmental domains
To realize key environmental topics influenced by the impacts of climate change at different impact rates
To learn about the historical development of sustainable management strategies and policies
To learn about the inter-/national policy frameworks, agreements and mechanisms for combating the climate change phenomenon
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Historical development of the sustainability concept, strategies and policy interventions
Hafta 2: Addressing climate change phenomenon at the transect of environmental sustainability
Hafta 3: Climate change science and assessment methods
Hafta 4: The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), its acts and contributions
Hafta 5: Climate models and projections for the future; Data collection and analysis methods for climate change assessments
Hafta 6: Impacts of climate change on biodiversity; Vulnerability and adaptation of ecosystems
Hafta 7: Impacts of climate change on water resources and quality; Sustainable water management; water scarcity and drought
Hafta 8: Mid-Term Exam; student projects/assignments distributions
Hafta 9: Energy and Carbon Footprint: The role of energy production in climate change; Climate policies for mitigating the carbon impact
Hafta 10: Urban heat island effect and climate adaptation in cities; Green infrastructure and urban planning for resilience
Hafta 11: Agricultural impacts of climate change; Risks on Food Security
Hafta 12: Climate change assessments within the Water-Energy-Food-Ecosystem (WEFE) Nexus
Hafta 13: Nature-Based Solutions (NBS) against climate change
Hafta 14: Student Assignments' Presentations
resources: Selected course materials will be provided/indicated during the lecture hours.

courses.name: SEMINAR
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to acquire students the skills of conducting literature research on any subject in the discipline of computer engineering, reporting the information obtained, and presenting report outputs.
courses.description: The course covers topic selection with the help of an advisor, effective literature research techniques, researching the selected topic, learning alternative solutions through literature research, comparing solutions, identifying the place of the subject in Computer Engineering, planning the implementation of the selected solution, preparing examples, structuring and detailing the report, determining the seminar presentation content, and completing the report and seminar presentation.
learning_outcomes: Explanation of a selected subject related to Computer Engineering
Researching solution strategies on the subject from the literature
Selecting the appropriate solution strategy among alternatives
Gaining the ability to prepare written reports on the subject
Gaining the ability to present the subject in front of the public with different presentation techniques

courses.name: PERSONAL STYLE AND IMAGE MANAGEMENT
courses.purpose: Image is not only how we look. Our image is a communication tool as institutional and individual. This course aims to help students create a positive first impression, improve their ability to manage others' perceptions, and develop an effective personal image that supports their professional life and career development.
courses.description: The course covers personal image and its components, including physical appearance, body language, nonverbal communication, speech, writing, email and internet culture, and social media presence. It also addresses personal personality recognition, professional image creation, clothing style, colors, accessories, body and face types, fabric selection, personal SWOT analysis, business etiquette, and communication in daily and professional life.
learning_outcomes: To understand personal image and the importance of personal image in Professional life
To understand the body language and its importance, to analyse the language of own body
To learn to selection of appropriate clothes and accessories in working life according to body and face types
To make the students think about the image that match with their career goals and make plans and improve themselves according to these goals
To learn doing personal SWOT analysis, to make them recognize themselves and Express themselves accurately in working life, social life, meetings, interviews
To learn email writing, speaking on the phone, business ethics

Hafta 2: Personal image and its importance, personal image according to personal differences, importance or first impression and appearence
Hafta 3: Personal image components. appearance (clothing, care , etc ) speech ( used words , tone , rhetoric and so on. )behavior ( attitude , friendliness , courtesy, manners , etc . )

courses.name: VOLUNTEERISM STUDIES
courses.purpose: The objective of this course is to provide students basic skills about organizing, coordinating and developing ties between society and university through education, knowledge, skills and efficiency they have gained all their lives. It is aimed to teach students being aware of social, economical and disadvantaged and vulnerable groups related issues such as human trafficking, migration, disabilities violence against women and children climate change etc. It is expected to raise awareness and willingness among students to take part in volunteer activities, to create visible positive changes in society by engaging them to take active roles in pre-planned volunteer works and let them present and share their efforts with their classmates and other related parties.

courses.name: HUMAN RIGHTS
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce basic human rights philosophy, nature, historical development, function, legal structure, human rights standards, mechanisms, and fundamental rights and freedoms of the individual. It also aims to analyze the ways in which allegations of human rights violations are handled.
courses.description: The course covers the philosophical foundations and principles of human rights, the development of classical and universal human rights thought, controversial aspects of human rights theory, universal, national, regional and local dynamics, constitutional order, human rights standards, mechanisms and institutions established for the protection of human rights, and current issues and discussions.
learning_outcomes: Able to identify key historical, political, legal and moral factors related to human rights.
Able to understand the concept of human rights, the important differences between the types of rights, the relationships between rights and other moral concepts.
Able to understand the similarities and differences in the essence and scope of human rights in the local, regional, national and international context.

Able to use the discourses of the mechanisms developed for the monitoring and implementation of human rights standards by grasping the main international declarations and treaties regulating human rights.
Able to develop analytical skills to question and evaluate human rights policies and practices at the international and national level.
Able to identify their possible roles in the promotion of human rights.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Basic concepts of human rights; first, second, third and fourth generation rights, Importance of human rights
Hafta 2: Historical development of human rights (period up to 19th century)

Hafta 3: Protection of human rights in the United Nations system United Nations Documents: Universal Declaration of Human Rights, Convention on Civil and Political Rights, Convention on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights) and Control Mechanisms

courses.name: COMPUTER ARCHITECTURE
courses.purpose: The main aim of this course is to introduce computer processors, memory organization and a brief view about the relationship between CPU and its peripherals. It is expected that students at the end of this course will be able to design a stored program computer using logic gates, flip-flops, and components from a given library of digital components.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamentals of computer architecture, including basic computer organization and design, CPU structure, instruction formats, addressing modes, microprogrammed control, computer arithmetic, pipeline and vector processing, input-output organization, memory organization, and multiprocessor systems. It also includes assembly language programming for the Basic Computer and concepts such as cache memory, virtual memory, DMA, and interrupt-driven I/O.
learning_outcomes: Understand the main concepts of computer architecture
Design and analyze the main functional units of a computer
Mention some of the strategies used in CISC and RISC architectures
Implement assembly programs that accomplish basic computational and I/O operations by using assembly of Basic Computer
Define some concepts of memory organization: main memory, cache memory and virtual memory
Illustrate the principles basic characteristics of multiprocessors, pipelining, vector processing, cache coherence
Provide knowledge about interfacing; I/O fundamentals such as handshaking, and I/O techniques such interrupt driven I/O and DMA
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, Combinatorial and Sequential Circuit review
Hafta 2: Basic Computer Org. and Design: Registers Transfers and Microoperations
Hafta 3: Basic Computer Org. and Design: Memory Reference Inst., Basic CPU Design
Hafta 4: Programming the Basic Computer, Introduction to Assembly Language
Hafta 5: Microprogrammed Control, Computer Arithmetic
Hafta 6: Central Processing Unit: Stacks, Instruction Format, Addressing Modes
Hafta 7: Review for Midterm and Solve Questions
Hafta 8: Central Processing Unit: Data Transfer Instructions, Program Control, RISC
Hafta 9: Pipeline and Vector Processing
Hafta 10: Input-Output Org.: Interfaces, Asynch. Data Transfer, Priority Interrupt, DMA
Hafta 11: Input-Output Organization: I/O Processor, Serial Communication
Hafta 12: Memory Organization: Main Memory, Cache Memory
Hafta 13: Memory Organization: Virtual Memory, Memory Management Hardware
Hafta 14: Multiprocessors

courses.name: NUMERICAL ANALYSIS
courses.purpose: This course is concerned with the mathematical derivation, description and analysis of obtaining numerical solutions of mathematical problems.
courses.description: The course covers core topics in numerical analysis, including Taylor polynomials, error analysis, root finding, fixed point iteration, solving systems of equations, LU-factorization, Gauss-Seidel and Jacobi methods, eigenvalue problems, interpolation and approximation, polynomial interpolation, least square approximation, numerical differentiation, and numerical integration.

Compose numerical solution of a given mathematical problem
Increase ability of reasoning and problem solving
Advice numerical approaches for solving problems using mathematical knowledge
Use methods in the field of numerical analysis for complex problems

courses.name: DATA ORGANIZATION AND MANAGEMENT
courses.purpose: The main objectives of this course are to discuss and teach the physical characteristics of storage media and organization and efficient processing of files.
courses.description: Introduction to data organization and management, data, information, and knowledge, data representation, XML technologies, data coding, storage devices and basic file operations, binary search and simple indexing, B+ tree, external sorting, introduction to DBMS, data normalization and SQL, and database design.
learning_outcomes: Use JSON and XML related technologies such as XML, XSD in application development.
Understand the basic characteristics of secondary storage characteristics
Understand state-of-the-art tree based file indexing structures, focusing on b+tree and its variants.
Understand external sorting algorithms
Use basic database functionalities.

courses.name: OBJECT ORIENTED ANALYSIS AND DESIGN
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to teach analysis and design methods and techniques on an object-oriented approach based on information systems. The main goal is to carry out studies to understand the techniques and methods of object-oriented analysis, design and programming that contribute to a safer, more reliable system. This course is structured to follow a general software development process that focuses on analysis and design aspects.
courses.description: Introduction to object-oriented programming and object technology principles, including Java basics, data types, program flow, classes and objects. The course covers object-oriented concepts such as inheritance, encapsulation, polymorphism, method overloading and overriding, packages, exception handling, and collections. It also introduces modeling and the software development process, business modeling, object-oriented analysis and design with UML, class and state modeling, design patterns, and a review of object-oriented programming.
learning_outcomes: Develop a working understanding of general object-oriented analysis and design processes.
To be able to analyze real world information systems and apply different methods.
Describe how a system interacts with its environment.
Develop the skills to determine which processes and OO techniques should be applied to a given project.
Develop an understanding of the application of OOAD practices from a software project management perspective.
Know how to use UML for notation.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Object Oriented Programming. Introduction to Object Technology Principles of Object Orientation Introduction to Java, Data Types, Program Flow, Classes and Objects
Hafta 2: Object-Oriented Concept. Overload and Override methods, Reuse the code in a class through inheritance, Use polymorphism to deal with objects in different forms, Organize objects in packages.
Hafta 3: Object Design and Programming with Java - 1. Inheritance, Encapsulation, Polymorphism, Method Overriding, Code Examples & Exercises
Hafta 4: Object Design and Programming with Java - 2. Java Exception Handling, Java Collections API

Hafta 5: Introducing Modeling and the Software Development Process. Describe the Object-Oriented Software Development (OOSD) process, modeling support, benefits of modeling software, and OOSD workflows.
Hafta 6: Fundamentals of Business Modeling. Introduction to Business Modeling Using the Unified Modeling Language, Business Modeling Process, From Business Models to System Models, Project Examples

Hafta 7: Object-oriented analysis with UML. Modeling System Behavior with Use Cases, Analysis and Design Overview, Architectural Analysis, Distribute Behavior to Classes, Describe the Analysis Class, Review the Analysis Model

Hafta 8: Object-oriented design with UML. Identify Design Elements, Identify design mechanisms, Incorporate Existing Design Elements, Describe the Run-time Architecture, Describe Distribution Patterns, Use-Case Design
Hafta 9: Refining the Class Design Model. Subsystem Design, Class Design: Class Structure, modeling states, class relationships, Implementation overview, Structure the implementation model
Hafta 10: Object-Oriented Analysis Case Study
Hafta 11: Modeling Object State Using State Machine Diagrams. Model object state, Describe the essential elements of a UML State Machine diagram

Hafta 14: Object Oriented Programming Review

courses.name: TURKISH LANGUAGE II
courses.purpose: The objective of Turkish Language Course is to enable the comprehension of the structural and operational features of Turkish; and to enable the acquisition of the proper and fine use of the language which is a means of written and spoken expression.
courses.description: This course covers Turkish language semantics, common mistakes in written and oral expression, and the correct use of Turkish in different forms of expression. It also introduces think-pieces, literary writings, and oral presentation skills.
learning_outcomes: To be able to use Turkish correctly, fluently and effectively in writing and talking.
To be able to distinguish the use of words and sentences with different semantic features.
To be able to recognize, distinguish and produce different types of written expression.
To be able to recognize and distinguish different types of oral expression, making oral presentations.
To be able to explain the important examples of written and oral expression types in Turkish literary history.
To be able to prevent, distinguish and correct errors that may be made in written and oral expression.

courses.name: SUSTAINABLE DESIGN OF ENGINEERING STRUCTURES
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop globally minded and socially aware engineers that are future agents of change, able to innovate and bring forward more holistic solutions that meet the needs of societies while protecting our natural resources. Through a balanced provision of fundamental and technical knowledge, practical application, students will be equipped with the necessary qualitative and quantitative analytical tools and skills, and further develop essential professional skills, that will enable them to tackle the evolving, complex local and global sustainable development issues and challenges.
courses.description: Introduction to sustainability concepts and challenges; sustainability in engineering practice; life cycle analysis and circular economy; pathways to net zero; environmental regulations; impact assessment; low carbon technologies; project management; renewable energy; measuring sustainability; environmental policy.
learning_outcomes: To understand the fundamentals of sustainability.
To learn tools are available to measure sustainability.
To learn Earth faces many environmental pressures that result from human exploitation of natural resources.
Develop to a more sustainable pattern of use.

courses.name: CLIMATE CHANGE AND SUSTAINABLE MANAGEMENT
courses.purpose: Teaching issues concerning causes, effects and prevention of climate changes and adaptation to climate changes, raising awareness to the climate change.
courses.description: The course covers climate and climatic factors, the greenhouse effect and greenhouse gases, the political dimension of climate change, climate change forecasts and models, the effects of climate change on sectors and society, mitigation and adaptation strategies, sustainability, national and international policies, and student presentations.
learning_outcomes: Learning basic concepts related with the climate change
To have an idea about the political dimensions of climate change
To understand the causes and effects of climate change
To have a general knowledge about combating with and adapting to climate change
Achieving the awareness to the importance of implementing the necessary engineering practices to combat with and adapt to the effects of climate change.

courses.name: THEORY OF COMPUTATION
courses.purpose: This course intends to equip students with knowledge and skills in computer languages, state machines, regular and context-free languages, Turing machines and computability.
courses.description: The course covers mathematical preliminaries, theorems and proofs; state machines and finite state automata; nondeterministic finite state automata; regular languages and regular expressions; non-regular languages and pumping lemma; context-free grammars; different forms of grammars and Chomsky normal form; push down automata; properties of context-free languages and non-context-free grammars; Turing machines; computability and decidability; undecidable languages; halting problem; reductions; complexity theory; and the classes P and NP.
learning_outcomes: Define proof techniques and apply reduction to given problems
Define regular languages and finite state machines
Define context-free languages and push-down automata
Define Turing Machines
Define P and NP classes

courses.name: PRINCIPLES OF ATATURK AND HISTORY OF THE TURKISH REVOLUTION I
courses.purpose: To get students comprehend development stages of Turkish community in transition from empire to nation state
courses.description: This course covers the basic concepts of the Turkish Revolution, the history of Western modernization, Ottoman-Turkish modernization until 1918, international competition in the 19th and 20th centuries and its effects on the Ottoman State, the Italo-Turkish and Balkan Wars, the process leading to World War I, military fronts in World War I, the Armistice of Mondros and the occupation process, developments in Anatolia after World War I, the Kuva-yı Milliye movement, Mustafa Kemal Atatürk's arrival in Samsun and the organization of the National Struggle, the opening of the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye, the establishment of the regular army, and the military struggle phase until the Great Offensive and the Mudanya Armistice.
learning_outcomes: To be able to explain general concepts that compose fundamentals of Turkish revolution
To be able to explain aims, methods and outcomes of westernization movements of the Ottoman Empire
To be able to comprehend developments that happened in domestic and foreign policies during the last period of the Ottoman Empire
To be able to understand impacts of the Great War to the Ottoman Empire
To be able to explicate politcal, economical, militarial and social events that happened during the National Struggle process

courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic operating system concepts such as processes and process synchronization, mutual exclusion, semaphores, process management and process scheduling, memory management, virtual memory and paging, interrupt structures and interrupt processing, device management, I/O systems and I/O processing.

Understand how data is stored, retrieved and maintained in different types of databases.
Gain experience with the existing database management systems.

courses.name: METHODS OF ARTIFICAL INTELLIGENCE
courses.purpose: The main objectives of this course are to discuss, teach and apply the methods, and search paradigms in AI. It also aims to increase students' analytical and theoretical thinking abilities so that they can solve problems efficiently.

courses.name: PRINCIPLES OF ATATURK AND HISTORY OF THE TURKISH REVOLUTION II
courses.purpose: To comprehend Turkish reforms and political, economical, social events in Türkiye from its establishment to present time

learning_outcomes: To be able to comprehend fundamental principles and characteristics of Turkish revolution
To be able to classify aim and social acquisitions of Turkish revolution
To be able to comprehend by which methods, in which conditions that Turkish revolution was realised and what kind of problems that were encountered in this process
To be able to interpret concepts such as principles of Ataturk, Ataturkism, Kemalism, Ataturkist thought system

Hafta 4: Revolutions and constitutions aimed at establishing a contemporary and secular legal system: Turkish Civil Code, 1921 and 1924 Constitutions
Hafta 5: Revolutions in the field of education: Law of Unification of Education, Alphabet Reform, University Reform, and other developments

courses.purpose: Aim of this course is, specifying the differences between program and software, which should be developed according to development methodologies and letting understand the steps of the methodology.

courses.name: PRINCIPLES OF EMBEDDED SYSTEMS
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to complement the knowledge base of the students with the programming, circuitry, components and design of embedded systems in order to provide them with capabilities they can use as soon as they graduate.
courses.description: This course covers general definitions, characteristics, properties and categories of embedded systems; examples and history of embedded systems; components and circuitry; microcontrollers and their comparison with microcomputers; electrical properties, power supply levels, noise, hazards and interconnection considerations; hardware design concepts; criteria for choosing a microcontroller; types of microcontrollers; Intel 8051 architecture and organization; internals of 8051; input/output, ports, serial communication, timers, counters and interrupts; and programming embedded systems using assembly language and other high-level tools.
learning_outcomes: Recognize the basic building blocks and components of embedded systems and microcontrollers.
Recognize and define the categories, properties, features, design criteria and example hardware of embedded systems.
Define and design digital systems and circuits of embedded systems, differentiate the properties of embedded systems and microcomputers.
Recognize and use state diagrams, tables, timing diagrams in embedded systems.
Analyze the low power design, electrical requirements and techniques of preventing electrical hazards.
Recognize and use the properties, features, instructions and circuit connections of an embedded system's microcontroller.
Locate, recognize and use the software and programming tools of embedded systems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: General definitions, characteristics, properties and categories of embedded systems.
Hafta 2: Examples of embedded systems, History of embedded systems and difficulties of designing embedded systems.
Hafta 3: Components of embedded systems, general properties of embedded system circuitry, examples of circuits and diagrams.
Hafta 4: Microcontrollers used in embedded systems. General properties of microcontrollers, a comparison of microcomputers and embedded systems.
Hafta 5: Electrical properties of embedded systems, power supply voltage levels, noise, hazards and interconnection considerations.
Hafta 6: Hardware design concepts. System clock, machine cycle, timers, crystals, speed versus heating and power consumption.
Hafta 7: Hardware design concepts: Circuit diagrams, conventions, semiconductors, gates, buses, integrated circuit connection pins, timing diagrams of pin voltages.
Hafta 8: Criteria of choosing a microcontroller for an embedded system, limited resource of embedded systems, CISC and RISC comparison.
Hafta 9: Types of microcontrollers used in embedded systems (for example wearable devices).
Hafta 10: Our target microcontroller: Intel 8051, basic organization, architecture, properties and on-chip memory
Hafta 11: Internals of 8051, timing and speed considerations, special function registers.
Hafta 12: Course and Lab revision, Mid-Term Exam questions and solutions
Hafta 13: Input/Output in 8051. Ports, serial communication, timers, counters, interrupts.

Hafta 14: Programming embedded systems, differences between software programming and embedded system programming, assembly language and other high level embedded system programming tools, writing secure by design code.

courses.name: DIGITAL TRANSFORMATION IN INDUSTRY
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to explain the basic principles and conceptual framework of digital transformation that started with the fourth industrial revolution. To understand the role and importance of digital transformation for businesses

courses.name: PHASE ARRAY RADAR TECHNOLOGIES
courses.purpose: Electronically Scanned Arrays (ESA) are antennas that provide controllable, agile, high-gain beams for areas such as radar and communications. Unlike antennas that require a reflector or wave-guide to steer the array beam, ESAs can steer the beam electronically in space without physical movement of the array. Within the scope of the course, the basic theory of ESAs will be explained and their applications in radar and communication systems will be introduced.
courses.description: The course covers the fundamentals of electronically scanned arrays and their use in radar and communication systems. It includes electromagnetic fields and waves, antenna systems, radar range equation, radar receivers, target detection, radar cross section, pulse compression, and electronically scanned array fundamentals in one and two dimensions. The course also includes project discussion and presentation.
learning_outcomes: To teach the fundamentals of ESAs.
To teach sub-array beam forming techniques.
To achieve beam optimization.
To achieve the electronic trackig of arrays in radar applications.
To achieve the electronic trackig of arrays in communication applications.

courses.name: INTRODUCTION TO ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
courses.purpose: The main objectives of this course are to discuss, teach and apply the methods, languages, and search paradigms in AI; and to increase the abilities of analytical and theoretical thinking of students so that they can solve problems efficiently.

courses.purpose: The student should be able to critically discuss an interaction design as a cultural object, be able to compare different user interfaces and know about principles of interaction design as a means for communication.

Skills be able to analyze a user interface from a communication perspective, to discuss a user interface from a historical perspective, to be able to select an appropriate interaction design pattern for a particular user interface situation

courses.purpose: The main objective of this course is to teach the main concepts and components of decision support systems such as descriptions and reasons to be developed by using example applications.
courses.description: This course covers the theory and practice of decision support systems, including expert systems, knowledge base creation, advanced SQL queries for decision making, knowledge discovery in databases, data mining, machine learning, optimization algorithms, stream mining, and time series applications.

courses.purpose: Main goal of this course is to understand and implement classical models and algorithms in data warehousing. courses.name: BASICS OF INTERNET OF THINGS
courses.purpose: The Internet of Things (IoT) is more than a smart or connected device. This course will show you what the IoT is and how it works in technical terms. The main aim of the course is to provide a basis for the usage, communication and security of Internet of things that are becoming more common. In addition to this, technical and conceptual structures will be explained about the cloud-based large data structures formed by many Internet of things and the analysis processes of large data.
courses.description: Introduction to IoT definitions, components, examples; IoT foundations, standards and ecosystems; IoT communication methods and low-power wireless networking; sensors, RFID tags and NFC cards; security issues in IoT; M2M communication principles; example IoT project; big data in IoT; use of cloud computing in IoT; cloud computing fundamentals; applications of IoT and cloud computing; IoT big data analytics; project presentations.
learning_outcomes: To understand the general principles of IoT and to develop applications.
Become familiar with the techniques and tools for automated capturing, processing, classifying and organizing knowledge.
To be able to develop web technologies, information acquisition and efficient deployment, using the ioT components in network and cloud-based business environments.
Understand the importance of cloud computing with ioT and its importance in various organizations.
To understand the general operating principles of sensors used for iot and to develop applications using different data communication methods.

courses.name: INTRODUCTION TO GRAPH THEORY
courses.purpose: Main goal of this course is to understand graph data structures and current graph search and optimization algorithms; eventually to increase the analytical thinking abilities of students for solving complex engineering problems.

courses.name: RECONFIGURABLE COMPUTING

It also includes applications of reconfigurable computing in areas such as parallel discrete event simulation, CPU design, security/encryption, mobile communication systems, pattern matching, neural networks, data acquisition systems, and health care systems.

courses.name: ENTREPRENEURSHIP AND INNOVATION
courses.purpose: Discuss the entrepreneurship and innovation trends for computer engineering in changing world structure. The role and importance of entrepreneurship in national economy and also the relation between entrepreneurship and innovation will be discussed. Have the necessary skills to realize innovation and entrepreneurship activities

courses.name: MICROPROCESSOR BASED SYSTEM DESIGN
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to understand the hardware and architectures based on the building block of many types of computers: the microprocessors; recognize machine-to-machine (M2) communication, applications in Internet of Things (IoT) and Internet of Everything (IoE); recognize the performance metrics of IoT and IoE server computers where information is saved and then searched.
courses.description: This course covers microprocessor-based hardware and architectures, machine-to-machine communication, IoT and IoE applications, embedded systems, communication protocols, multicore and multitasking processor architectures, cloud computing in IoT/IoE, and project-based design and evaluation.
learning_outcomes: Recognize the features and architectures of processors.
Recognize the hyperthreading and multi-core architectures of modern processors and acquire the capability to exploit their benefits.
Recognize the shortcomings of resource stricken embedded systems, RFID tags and sensors found in IoT devices.
Recognize the characteristics of microprocessor based devices and their communication protocols found in IoT and IoE infrastructures.
Recognize the IoT and IoE applications and acquire the capability of developing similar applications.
Recognize and compare the properties and features of different multi-tasking, multi-user and multi-core processor servers, where the IoT and IoE entities get connected.

courses.name: INTRODUCTION TO SUSTAINABLE ENERGY SYSTEMS
courses.purpose: The purpose of the course is to provide knowledge on global energy and environmental problems and basic information on sustainable energy technologies. Applications include modeling and simulation of acoustic, elastic and electromagnetic waves.
learning_outcomes: Ability to use and express main partial differential equations (wave, heat-diffusion, Laplace, Poisson, Helmholtz).

Ability to construct and apply the fundamental solutions for main partial differential equations.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Main partial differential equations of mathematical physics: transport equation, Wave equation, heat-diffusion equation, the Laplace equation and Helholtz equation.

Hafta 2: Initial value and initial boundary value problems for the wave and heat-diffusion equations. Boundary value problems for the Laplace, Poisson and Helmholtz equations. Correctness of the problem statements.
Hafta 4: The solution of the initial value problem for 1D,2D and 3D wave equations by d' Alambert, Poisson and Kirchhoff formulas.

Hafta 5: Eigenvalue and eigenfunction problems for the second order ordinary differential equations. Finding all eigenvalues and corresponding tho them eigenfunctions. Properties of eigenvalues and eigenfunctions.
Hafta 6: Separation of the variables method for solving the initial boundary value problems for homogeneous wave and heat-diffusion equations.
Hafta 7: The Fourier series expansion method for the construction of the solutions of the initial boundary value problems for inhomogeneous wave and heat-diffusion equations.
Hafta 8: Solving boundary value problems for the Laplace equation by the Fourier series expansion method.
Hafta 9: The Dirac delta function and its properties. The fundamental solution of the initial value problems for the ordinary differential equations. The Fourier transformation and its properties.
Hafta 11: Conctruction of the fundamental solutions of the initial value problem for 1D, 2D and 3D wave equations. Applications of the fundamental solutions.
Hafta 12: Conctruction of the fundamental solutions of the initial value problem for 1D, 2D and 3D heat-diffusion equations. Applications of the fundamental solutions.
Hafta 13: The fundamental solutions for the Laplace and Helmholtz equations. Applications of the fundamental solutions.
Hafta 14: Modeling and simulation of acoustic, elastic and electromagnetic waves.

courses.name: QUALITY MANAGEMENT
courses.purpose: The purpose of the course is, generating the knowledge background for when organization establish a quality management system, to define the main stages, to constitute the infrastructions, to carry out objectives by using consistent methods and technichs.
courses.description: Overview of quality management, the approach of total quality management, the principles of quality management, DMCA cycle; worldwide quality management systems such as ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 18001 and EFQM; planning of a quality management system including quality policy, quality strategies, quality objectives, strategic planning, SWOT analyses, process design and documentation; communications of internal and external interested parties, use of resources, personnel responsibilities and authorities, training activities, and customer/supplier relationships; monitoring, measuring and improving the efficiency of a quality management system through data collection, evaluation, problem identification, root cause analysis, problem solving techniques, and continuous improvement methods such as quality circles and Kaizen.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to define, establish, implement a QM System, to monitor, measure and improvement efficiency of this system.

Students will be able to design, develop, implement and improve systems under realistic conditions such as cost, environment, sustainability, manufacturability, ethics, health, safety and social problems.
Students will be able to be aware, make amends, solve problems and improve the weaknesses of quality systems.
Students will be able to be consciousness of using resources efficiently, competence of managing projects, when a quality management system is established, implemented and improved.
Students will be able to implement professional knowledge in various quality management standarts / models.

Hafta 2: World-widespread QM Systems (ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 18001, EFQM, etc.)

Hafta 13: Monitoring, Measuring and Improving Efficiency of QM System. Problem solving technichs.
